item.title: Judge declares another mistrial in Harvey Weinstein New York rape case
item.description: It marks the third time a New York jury has considered the case against the 74-year-old disgraced film mogul.

item.title: French national shows symptoms on return from hantavirus-hit ship
item.description: Five passengers of the MV Hondius will be quarantined in Paris "until further notice", France's prime minister says.

item.title: Trump and Xi conclude 'very successful' talks but no deals confirmed
item.description: There were plenty of choreographed ceremonies but no trade breakthroughs after the two-day visit.

item.title: Drake gets personal with surprise three-album drop after Kendrick beef
item.description: These are the first albums the Canadian rapper has dropped since his beef with Kendrick Lamar escalated in 2024.

item.title: Badenoch 'flattered' by Thatcher comparison from Nicki Minaj
item.description: Badenoch says she is a fan of the Trinidad and Tobago-born rapper's song Starship after being endorsed by her.

item.title: Is this the real face of Anne Boleyn?
item.description: A computer science team believes they have discovered a previously unknown sketch of King Henry VIII's second wife - but not everyone is convinced.

item.title: US to seek death penalty for suspect in killing of Israeli embassy staff members
item.description: The US government alleges that Elias Rodriguez shot and killed a young Jewish couple at an event in Washington last May.

item.title: WhatsApp launches totally private 'incognito' conversations with its AI chatbot
item.description: A cyber security expert says deleting chat history could lead to a lack of accountability if things go wrong.

item.title: HMRC to use AI from British tech firm to spot fraud and tax return errors
item.description: Quantexa, a financial data platform, won the £175m contract to spot fraud and tax return errors.

item.title: 'Don't swim' at 12 of 14 river bathing sites, as more locations announced
item.description: Too much bacteria linked to faeces found at almost all England's designated river bathing sites

item.title: What is hantavirus and how does it spread?
item.description: The Andes strain of the virus, which can rarely be passed from person to person, has been found among passengers of a cruise ship.

item.title: Middlesbrough want Southampton kicked out of play-offs over Spygate
item.description: Middlesbrough demand Southampton's expulsion from the Championship play-offs over spying allegations.

item.title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
item.description: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

item.title: Suicide forum fined £950,000 for not blocking UK users
item.description: The regulator said the forum had not done enough to protect UK users - but critics accuse Ofcom of acting too slowly.

item.title: Harry and Meghan to produce Afghan war film for Netflix
item.description: The couple's production company will make a film based on a true story of British troops under siege.

item.title: What China critics in Maga movement make of Trump's Beijing trip
item.description: Trump’s softer approach towards China is one that experts say will likely trickle down to the wider MAGA base.

item.title: Trump warns Taiwan against declaring independence, hours after summit with China's Xi
item.description: The US president says he wants Beijing and Taipei to "cool down" tensions over the self-governing island.

item.title: Couple took underage sons abroad for marriage
item.description: They are handed suspended prison sentences after taking their sons to be married in Pakistan.

item.title: 'We're right on track,' says Streeting as key target for hospital waiting times hit
item.description: Government his its interim target of 65% of patients in England being treated within 18 weeks.

item.title: Beckhams become billionaires as Oasis make rich list for first time
item.description: The list is based off the paper's estimates of the minimum wealth of Britain’s 350 richest people or families.

item.title: 'We're living in a shed because of river pollution'
item.description: Jane and Tony Coyle spent seven years waiting for planning permission due to River Lugg pollution.

item.title: Daily pill to help keep weight off after stopping obesity jabs
item.description: The tablet - orforglipron - is available in the US and could soon launch in the UK.

item.title: 'Pretty miraculous' - 11 rescued after pilot ditches plane in Atlantic
item.description: The survivors spent about five hours on a life raft before being rescued by the US Air Force off Florida's coast.

item.title: Teenager's family 'heartbroken' by meningitis death
item.description: The family of sixth form pupil Lewis Waters post a tribute following his death.

item.title: More than 70 million warnings sent to people seeking child abuse material
item.description: The messages highlight the illegality of what people are searching for online and direct them towards help.

item.title: X pledges quicker action on hate and terror content in the UK
item.description: Ofcom said the commitments were of particular importance after recent crimes targeting Jewish communities in the UK.

item.title: Australia soars into Eurovision final as UK song debuts
item.description: Delta Goodrem is now a favourite to win Eurovision, following a spectacular semi-final performance.

item.title: Olly Murs in tears as he completes endurance feat
item.description: He crossed the finish line at the London Stadium in Stratford after covering about 250 miles.

item.title: Sydney dentist's patients warned of viruses including HIV
item.description: Authorities urged patients to get tested due to "poor infection control practices" at the Australian clinic.

item.title: How Rayner, Streeting and Burnham weakened PM in 12 hours of political drama
item.description: Three interventions by three senior Labour politicians made for an unforgettable day in British politics.

item.title: Farage paid for £1.4m house with I'm a Celebrity cash, says Reform
item.description: The Reform UK leader is being investigated by Parliament's standards watchdog over a £5m gift from a billionaire.

item.title: Ukrainian man denies role in Starmer arson plot, court hears
item.description: Petro Pochynok is on trial along with two others for allegedly plotting to set fire to a car and two houses linked to the prime minister.

item.title: Eleven 'far-right agitators' banned from UK ahead of rally, government says
item.description: Saturday's rally has been organised by anti-Islam activist Stephen Yaxley-Lennon, better known as Tommy Robinson.

item.title: Is it safe to swim at England's bathing sites?
item.description: Signs warning people not to swim are in place at almost all of England's official inland river bathing sites due to concerns the water could be unsafe

item.title: Kohli will skip World Cup if he has to prove worth
item.description: India's Virat Kohli says he will not play in the 2027 Cricket World Cup if he needs to prove his worth to the team.

item.title: British Gas pays £20m over prepayment meter force-fitting scandal
item.description: The regulator say the energy supplier breached licence conditions aimed at protecting customers in vulnerable situations.

item.title: Burnham cleared to run for selection in pivotal by-election
item.description: If the Greater Manchester mayor goes on to the win the by-election, he would be able to launch a leadership challenge against the PM.

item.title: British firm vying to build next Red Arrows jets collapses
item.description: Aeralis was hoping the UK government would award it a contract to replace the Hawk jets currently flown by the RAF.

item.title: I'm a passionate lad and I love it at Everton - Pickford
item.description: Jordan Pickford speaks to Wayne Rooney about Everton, England at the World Cup and becoming number one.

item.title: White-tailed eagles to be released in Exmoor despite farmer warnings
item.description: Some farmers fear the reintroduction of the UK's biggest bird of prey will threaten their livestock.

item.title: New York Times defends journalist after Israel threatens to sue
item.description: Israel's prime minister says he has ordered legal action over an article which alleged Israeli security officials raped Palestinian detainees.

item.title: UK Eurovision act: The BBC gave me a stress test to check I could cope under pressure
item.description: "It could go well or completely wrong, I'm just here for the ride," says song contest hopeful Sam Battle.

item.title: Inside the secretive and lucrative world of orchid breeding
item.description: It can take a decade to bring a new orchid to market, so breeders keep their hi-tech processes secret.

item.title: Tributes to Gogglebox star who died after illness
item.description: A tribute to Ken Harwood is expected to feature in an episode of the Channel 4 show later.

item.title: Forest seal play-off spot with Lightning win
item.description: Nottingham Forest reach the Netball Super League play-offs for the first time in their history with a 76-65 victory over leaders Loughborough Lightning.

item.title: UK backs human rights plan to accelerate illegal migration removals
item.description: The agreement pushes for a new approach to the continent's main human rights treaty to tackle modern migration pressures.

item.title: US hotel owners expected a World Cup boom - so far it hasn't happened
item.description: An industry body survey found hotels in World Cup host cities see the tournament as a "non-event".

item.title: Handling nerves & speaking Welsh - Packer on England's Grand Slam bid
item.description: Lucy Packer is the opposite of every scrum-half stereotype - but she is playing a key role in England's pursuit of a fifth straight Six Nations Grand Slam.

item.title: Thousands of Waymos recalled after robotaxi swept into a creek
item.description: The voluntary recall follows an incident on 20 April where an empty Waymo car entered a flooded road in San Antonio, Texas.

item.title: UK alcohol deaths fall for first time since Covid pandemic
item.description: Experts say the “modest reduction” is “not cause for complacency”, with calls to redouble efforts to slash deaths further.

item.title: Claim, counter-claim and tech's seedy side exposed: Five things we learned in the Musk-Altman trial
item.description: As the jury deliberates, this is what we found out during the weeks-long trial with two tech titans at its heart.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire, US state department says
item.description: President Trump announced the truce last month, but Israel and Hezbollah have continued to exchange fire since then.

item.title: ICE releases wife of US soldier and Afghanistan veteran from detention
item.description: Deisy Rivera Ortega was taken into custody at an immigration appointment in Texas last month.

item.title: UK's biggest ever environmental pollution claim reaches High Court
item.description: One of the UK's largest chicken producers and a water company accused of polluting three rivers including the River Wye

item.title: New outbreak of Ebola kills 65 in eastern DR Congo
item.description: Africa's top health agency says around 246 cases have been reported.

item.title: UK borrowing costs rise and pound falls as leadership drama continues
item.description: Analysts say the moves have been fuelled by concerns a Burnham-led government would increase government borrowing.

item.title: Who is James Murray, the new health secretary replacing Wes Streeting?
item.description: What are the most pressing priorities for the new minister in charge of health?

item.title: AI vigilante trap snares alleged paedophile ex-teacher in France
item.description: The 66-year-old handed himself in after an influencer used AI to pose as a 14-year-old girl and broadcast their conversation online.

item.title: £20m mystery gift buys London Zoo new hospital where you can watch vets work
item.description: Visitors will be able to watch live veterinary procedures inside a state-of-the-art new animal hospital.

item.title: 'Millions' of pounds saved by replacing Palantir tech in refugee system
item.description: The government said its in-house IT system was "more flexible" while meeting "high standards" of security.

item.title: Electric motorbike teen detained for killing gran on zebra crossing
item.description: Billy Stokoe is detained for causing the death by dangerous driving of Gloria Stephenson.

item.title: Search for new James Bond officially kicks off as auditions begin
item.description: Callum Turner, Jacob Elordi and Harris Dickinson are among the frontrunners to replace Daniel Craig.

item.title: Massive Alaska megatsunami was second largest ever recorded
item.description: New research suggests glacier melt driven by climate change is increasing the risk of giant waves.

item.title: Vingegaard claims impressive Giro stage win
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard puts on an impressive ride to win stage seven of the Giro d'Italia and complete his set of Grand Tour stage victories.

item.title: Five Italians die during cave scuba dive in Maldives
item.description: Four of the Italians were part of a team from the University of Genoa.

item.title: Posh sandwich has more salt than nearly five cheeseburgers
item.description: Action on Salt & Sugar said people should not be exposed to a "hidden health risk every time they buy lunch".

item.title: How are countries responding to hantavirus?
item.description: The UK, US and EU are asking all citizens returning home from the virus-hit MV Hondius to self-isolate for about six weeks.

item.title: Man arrested after referee Beaton and family put under police surveillance
item.description: The SFA say referee John Beaton and his family were put "under police surveillance" in the wake of a recent penalty controversy, with police confirming a man had been arrested in connection with a data leak.

item.title: Is this actually what Anne Boleyn looked like?
item.description: An algorithm has suggested images of what were thought to be Anne Boleyn might not be.

item.title: How worried should we be about hantavirus?
item.description: Passengers potentially exposed to hantavirus are being repatriated, so what is the risk to the wider public?

item.title: Heathrow rival could lead expansion, watchdog says
item.description: The aviation watchdog is considering new rules for Heathrow ahead of its possible expansion.

item.title: Carrick in talks over two-year Man Utd contract
item.description: Manchester United are in negotiations with Michael Carrick over a two-year deal to become head coach on a permanent basis.

item.title: Everybody wants Hearts to win - Celtic boss O'Neill
item.description: Celtic manager Martin O'Neill believes "everybody wants Hearts to win" as he prepares his side for Saturday's Scottish Premiership title decider.

item.title: James Corden names 'very, very, very bad' Gavin and Stacey episode
item.description: The plot involved a phone call-related misunderstanding between the titular characters.

item.title: What does Makerfield make of by-election and can Burnham win?
item.description: Andy Burnham says he will look to stand in the constituency, after Labour MP Josh Simons announces his resignation.

item.title: Police criticise Celtic over failure to plan title party for fans
item.description: Celtic fans have previously been involved in anti-social behaviour after unofficial mass gatherings in Glasgow.

item.title: Mandelson papers being withheld, says intelligence watchdog
item.description: Responding to the criticism the government said it would "publish as much material as we can as soon as possible".

item.title: Farke says Leeds must match his ambition
item.description: Leeds head coach Daniel Farke says he is "not the right choice" to remain in charge of the club if they fail to match his ambition in the summer.

item.title: AI could put people off tech jobs and hurt the economy, warns Raspberry Pi boss
item.description: Eben Upton warns against claims that Artificial Intelligence will destroy vast numbers of computing roles over the coming years.

item.title: Prisoner swap goes ahead as Kyiv mourns 24 killed in Russian strike on flats
item.description: Among the victims in Kyiv was 12-year-old Lyubava Yakovleva, whose father was killed during the war.

item.title: MPs call for sunbed advertising ban to help prevent skin cancer
item.description: A report by a cross-party group of MPs has found the majority of skin cancer cases are preventable.

item.title: NHS staff accessed Southport victims' records 'inappropriately', hospital trust admits
item.description: The victims affected by the data breach were not told for nearly two years.
